verse.arabic_text: وَذَا النُّونِ إِذ ذَّهَبَ مُغَاضِبًا فَظَنَّ أَن لَّن نَّقْدِرَ عَلَيْهِ فَنَادَىٰ فِي الظُّلُمَاتِ أَن لَّا إِلَٰهَ إِلَّا أَنتَ سُبْحَانَكَ إِنِّي كُنتُ مِنَ الظَّالِمِينَ
verse.translation: And [mention] the man of the fish, when he went off in anger and thought that We would not decree [anything] upon him. And he called out within the darknesses, 'There is no deity except You; exalted are You. Indeed, I have been of the wrongdoers.'

theme_question.correct: The power of sincere repentance and acknowledging Allah's Oneness in times of distress.
options: The power of sincere repentance and acknowledging Allah's Oneness in times of distress.
The importance of physical strength in overcoming challenges.
The prohibition of consuming fish from the sea.
A detailed guide on navigating the ocean depths.

educational_notes.verse_context: This verse contains the powerful supplication of Prophet Yunus (Jonah) when he was in the belly of the whale. After leaving his people in anger without Allah's permission, he was swallowed by a great fish. In the triple darkness—of the night, the sea, and the whale's belly—he called out to Allah, acknowledging his mistake and affirming Allah's absolute perfection and Oneness.
educational_notes.theme_explanation: The theme is profound repentance (Tawbah) and the centrality of Tawhid (Oneness of Allah). The dua of Yunus (a.s.) is a comprehensive formula for seeking forgiveness. It begins with affirming Tawhid ('La ilaha illa Anta'), glorifying Allah ('Subhanak'), and admitting one's own wrongdoing ('inni kuntu minaz-zalimin'). The Prophet Muhammad (ﷺ) said that no Muslim supplicates with this dua for anything, except that Allah will answer him.
educational_notes.surah_overview: Surah Al-Anbiya (The Prophets), a Meccan surah, emphasizes the fundamental message of all prophets: the Oneness of Allah (Tawhid). It recounts the stories of numerous prophets, including Ibrahim, Lut, Ishaq, Yaqub, Nuh, Dawud, Sulaiman, Ayyub, Ismail, Idris, Dhul-Kifl, Yunus, Zakariyya, and Yahya, highlighting their struggles and their unwavering faith. The surah serves as a reminder that the Day of Judgment is near and people are heedless.
